warnings: mode=adiabatic: each oxidation state was geometry-optimized separately and the potential uses ΔE between the relaxed geometries (includes reorganization). It does NOT include ZPE/thermal/entropy (ΔE used in place of ΔG); use mode=freq for a true ΔG.

Solvation is implicit/continuum only (or gas phase) — no explicit solvent shell and no Born–Haber solvation cycle. Semi-empirical redox potentials are screening-grade (≈±0.3–0.5 V); even DFT here omits the explicit-solvation correction. Treat as a screening estimate.

E°(vs SHE) uses E_abs(SHE) = 4.281 V. The absolute SHE potential is convention-dependent (literature ≈4.28–4.44 V); this is a systematic offset on the reported potential. Override with --e-abs-she to match your reference convention.

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

prose:
Property: Standard redox potential (E°) for the 0 → –1 couple of tetracyanoethylene (TCNE).
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding), no Gaussian basis.
Geometry provenance: adiabatic protocol — each oxidation state was geometry-optimized at GFN2-xTB; the potential uses the ΔE between those two relaxed geometries (GFN2-xTB//GFN2-xTB).
Solvent / environment: implicit water, xtb’s GBSA-family continuum model (no explicit solvent molecules).
Charge & multiplicity: oxidized state 0/1 (singlet), reduced state –1/2 (doublet).
Reference electrode: SHE with |Eₐᵦₛ(SHE)| = 4.281 V.
Software: xtb backend as wrapped by chemkit redox-potential skill (2026-06-25 build).
Convergence: both oxidized and reduced geometry optimizations reported converged; integrity check status ok, trustworthy = true.
Live log: /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/redox-potential-validation/tetracyanoethylene/20260625-115621_tetracyanoethylene_redox_potential/redox_20260625-115629.out (written live during run).

Headline result (computed): 5.5479485 V vs SHE.

Caveats (echoed verbatim from engine): see warnings list above.

Reproducibility: command line recorded in the JSON (`cli_invocation`); optimized geometries saved at the paths listed in oxidized_state.optimized_xyz and reduced_state.optimized_xyz.
